preamble: Fortition (hardening) of the voiceless velar fricative [x] to the voiceless velar stop [k] is a documented shift. It frequently occurs as a result of language contact—when a language lacking the fricative phoneme adapts loanwords—or as part of a broader fortition process in specific phonetic environments.

examples.note: Perso-Arabic loanwords with [x] are often adapted as the aspirated stop [kʰ] by speakers who lack [x] in their native inventory.
Occasional fortition in specific dialectal developments from Proto-Germanic.

sources: Ohala, J. J. (1993). The Phonetics of Sound Change.
